creator: gavin@FreeBSD.org
text:
Alexey Vesnin wrote:
> Good daytime! "Cable problem" is not a problem here - most of CF to IDE 
> adapters are acting like that. The true problem is that ad3 - which 
> should be Gigabyte i-RAM - is not there AT ALL. I read 'man ata' and the 
> sata-150 chip is in supported devices list, but.... There's no ripple 
> about Gigabyte i-Ram installed on sata0 slot ( if I plug a hdd there - 
> device ad3 is up and running ). CF adapter that is not working tries to 
> operate in high-speed UDMA mode and it tries forever, both i-ram and CF 
> adapter are detected, probed and are working pretty fine in Windows and 
> Linux'es. I doubt that there's some incorrect sata controller probing 
> occures, according to my brief look and some experiments with code in 
> src/sys/dev/ata...

Hi,

Are you able to test a more up to date version of FreeBSD?  (ideally
7.1-RELEASE).  A lot of changes have been made to the ATA code since
6.x.

Also, I think we'll need to see a verbose dmesg from the system before
there's a chance of figuring out what is wrong,

Thanks,

Gavin
